briefdescription.para: This function validates the XAsu_AesKeyObject structure parameters.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XAsu_AesKeyObject structure which holds the key object parameters.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS, if validation of key object parameters is successfully.
XASU_INVALID_ARGUMENT, if any argument is invalid.

inbodydescription.para: Validate that exactly one of KeyAddress or KeyId is provided.

briefdescription.para: This function validates AES mode for AAD.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS, if AES mode for AAD is valid.
XASU_INVALID_ARGUMENT, if AES mode for AAD is invalid.

briefdescription.para: This function validates AAD length.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XAsu_AesParams structure which contains AES AES input and output parameters.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS, if AAD length is valid.
XASU_INVALID_ARGUMENT, if AAD length is invalid.

inbodydescription.para: For standard modes, AAD length and address must be zero.

In MAC only modes (AES-CMAC), AAD is mandatory: AadLen and AadAddr must be non-zero, within the allowed DMA limit, and block-size aligned for all intermediate transfers (unaligned length allowed only for final transfer).
In GCM/CCM modes, AAD is optional: zero AAD is permitted when valid input/output data and data length are provided, allowing payload-only, AAD-only, or combined operation.

briefdescription.para: This function validates AES Engine mode.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XAsu_AesParams structure which contains AES AES input and output parameters.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS, if AES Engine mode is valid.
XASU_INVALID_ARGUMENT, if mode is invalid.

briefdescription.para: This function validates operation flags for AES CCM mode.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XAsu_AesParams structure which contains AES AES input and output parameters.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS, if flags are valid for AES CCM mode.
XASU_INVALID_ARGUMENT, if flags are invalid.

inbodydescription.para: For CCM, INIT combined with UPDATE but without FINAL is not allowed. AadLen/DataLen on INIT encode the B0 block totals; if UPDATE is also set without FINAL, they carry per-chunk sizes instead, producing an invalid tag. INIT|UPDATE|FINAL (single-shot) is valid since totals equal chunk sizes.

briefdescription.para: This function validates Data length.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XAsu_AesParams structure which contains AES AES input and output parameters.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS, if Data length is valid.
XASU_INVALID_ARGUMENT, if Data length is invalid.

inbodydescription.para: For standard modes, data length and addresses must be present and length must be within maximum DMA limit.
If Data Length is zero, addresses must also be zero.
If Data length is non-zero, both input and output addresses must be valid and data length must be within maximum DMA limit. AES-CMAC engine mode must not allow data updates.

briefdescription.para: Internal helper that executes a single AES client-side KAT.

detaileddescription.para: It sends an AES operation request to ASUFW, busy-waits for the response, and compares the result against expected vectors.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the KAT vector set describing the test case.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS, if the KAT passes.
XST_FAILURE, if the AES operation fails or output comparison fails.

inbodydescription.para: For authenticated decrypt, pre-fill the tag buffer with the expected tag so the server can verify it.
Configure key object.
Configure AES parameters for single-shot operation.
Configure client parameters for synchronous operation.
Send AES operation request to ASUFW.
Busy-wait for ASUFW response.
Check operation status from callback.
Compare output data with expected result.
For CCM, verify server-reported AdditionalStatus and compare generated tag with expected tag on encrypt.
For encrypt, the server writes the computed tag into the tag buffer and reports TAG_READ. Verify that status, then compare the tag.
For decrypt, the server verifies the pre-filled tag internally and reports TAG_MATCHED on success.
Zeroize local output buffers.

briefdescription.para: This function sends command to ASUFW to perform AES encryption/decryption operation on a given payload data with specified AES mode.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XAsu_ClientParams structure which holds the client input parameters.
Pointer to XAsu_AesParams structure which holds the parameters of AES input arguments.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS, if IPI request to ASU is sent successfully.
XASU_INVALID_ARGUMENT, if any argument is invalid.
XASU_QUEUE_FULL, if Queue buffer is full.
XST_FAILURE, if sending an IPI request to ASU fails.
XASU_CLIENT_CTX_NOT_CREATED, if client context is not created.
XASU_REQUEST_INPROGRESS, if split request already in progress.
XASU_FAIL_SAVE_CTX, if saving context fails.
XASU_INVALID_UNIQUE_ID, if received Queue ID is invalid.
XASU_FREE_CTX_FAIL, if freeing context fails.

simplesect.para:
Verify the additional status if operation flag is set to XASU_FINISH.
XASU_AES_TAG_READ, if the encryption operation is successfully done.
XASU_AES_TAG_MATCHED, if the decryption operation is successfully done.
Any other value shall be treated as failure.

inbodydescription.para: Validate the input parameters.
Validate the operation flags for AES CCM mode to ensure all the flags are set.
Validate that exactly one of IvAddr or IvId is provided.
Validate required parameters for AES initialization operation.
Validate AES operation type.
Validate AES engine mode.
Validate AES key object structure parameters.
Validate required parameters for AES update operation.

Both AAD and InputData/OutputData address and lengths cannot be zero at once. The minimum length of plaintext/AAD must be at least 1 byte, while the maximum length can be 0x1FFFFFFC bytes, which is the ASU DMA's maximum supported data transfer length.
Validate the input data alignment.
Validate IsLast flag.
Validate tag for AES final operation.
Validate required parameters for AES update operation.
Handle context operation.
Cleanup on FINAL operation.
Create command header.
Update request buffer and send an IPI request to ASU.

briefdescription.para: This function sends command to perform AES Known Answer Tests (KAT's).

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XAsu_ClientParams structure which holds the client input parameters.
AES supported KAT mode (for standard mode : XASU_AES_ECB_MODE, for mac mode : XASU_AES_GCM_MODE, and for DPA mode : XASU_AES_KAT_DPA_MODE in CTR engine mode)

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS, if IPI request to ASU is sent successfully.
XASU_INVALID_ARGUMENT, if any argument is invalid.
XASU_QUEUE_FULL, if Queue buffer is full.
XST_FAILURE, if sending an IPI request to ASU fails.
XASU_INVALID_UNIQUE_ID, if received Queue ID is invalid.

inbodydescription.para: Validate input parameters.
Validate KAT mode.
Generate unique ID and register the callback function.
Create command header.
Update request buffer and send an IPI request to ASU.

briefdescription.para: Callback handler for AES client KAT operations.

detaileddescription.para: Sets the completion flag and captures the operation status via per-invocation state.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the caller's XAsu_AesKatCbState on stack.

briefdescription.para: This function runs AES-CBC encryption KAT from the client side.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS, if AES-CBC encryption KAT passes.
XST_FAILURE, if the KAT fails.

briefdescription.para: This function runs AES-CBC decryption KAT from the client side.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS, if AES-CBC decryption KAT passes.
XST_FAILURE, if the KAT fails.

briefdescription.para: This function runs AES-CCM encryption KAT from the client side.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS, if AES-CCM encryption KAT passes.
XST_FAILURE, if the KAT fails.

briefdescription.para: This function runs AES-CCM decryption KAT from the client side.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS, if AES-CCM decryption KAT passes.
XST_FAILURE, if the KAT fails.

briefdescription.para: AES KAT data length.
AES KAT key length.
AES CBC IV length.
AES CCM nonce length.
AES CCM AAD length.
AES CCM tag length.
